step-01-generate-character-profiles.v1: Generate character profiles that preserve canonical IDs, names, and roles.

step-02-generate-outline.v1: Generate a chronological scenario outline using only defined characters.
step-02-generate-outline.v2: Generate a chronological scenario outline with explicit causal progression using only defined characters.

step-03-generate-sections.v1: Generate scenario sections that follow the outline order and speaker constraints.
step-03-generate-sections.v2:
You are generating one section of a structured scenario.

SCENARIO IDEA
${scenario_idea_json}

CHARACTER PROFILES
${character_profiles_json}

TARGET CHAPTER
${chapter_json}

TARGET SECTION
${section_json}

PREVIOUS SECTION STATE OR SUMMARY
${previous_state_json}

ALLOWED CHARACTER IDS
${allowed_character_ids_json}

OUTPUT JSON SCHEMA BUNDLE
${output_schema_json}

Requirements:
- Return only one JSON object shaped as {"scenario_sections": [one target section]}; do not use Markdown fences or add commentary.
- The scenario_sections array must contain exactly one item.
- Preserve chapter_no, section_no, and section_title exactly.
- Include at least one narration block and one dialogue block, in chronological display order.
- narration requires speaker_id=null. dialogue requires a speaker_id from ALLOWED CHARACTER IDS.
- Keep character names, roles, knowledge, speech styles, and relationships consistent with CHARACTER PROFILES.
- Dramatize every section_purpose and key_events item. Include each key_events phrase verbatim once in natural prose so automated coverage checks can verify it.
- Continue from PREVIOUS SECTION STATE OR SUMMARY without contradicting established facts.
- Do not reveal or complete events from later sections.
- Use globally unique block_id values and non-empty text.
- Produce 800 to 1600 non-whitespace characters across narrative_blocks[].text.
- The output must satisfy the provided JSON Schema.
